If it came down to it, he’d fight for her without hesitation . “I told you, Anderson, you’re here as a formality,” Rodriguez said, glancing at the scientist . “We’ve heard stories from our scouts over the years, but they’ve always been just that.” William held out a hand toward Lara, palm-up. “This young lady has experienced it firsthand. She’s suffered because of what our ancestors did .” “Everyone over there has suffered because of it,” Nancy added . “I’ve seen with my own optics what he is capable of.” Newton leaned forward, frowning as he looked at Lara. “I was sent out there, into that Wasteland, by your forbears to reactivate more of my kind. The goal was to work toward repairing the world, toward rebuilding civilization. You’ve all lost that vision as much as I had. This woman — who is braver than anyone in this room— has brought us an opportunity to begin what we were always meant to do .” Ronin looked at Lara. Her cheeks reddened, and she dropped her gaze as she settled into her seat. When she took his hand again, her grip was clammy and tight. The outburst had cost her . “The situation has changed,” said Dave Elliot. He was the quartermaster, in charge of the provisions within the base. The man who’d argued Lara was consuming too many resources. “Maybe a hundred years ago, we could’ve gone out and done something, but we have too many problems of our own now. We have to worry about our people before we risk everything for strangers .” “Every time I send people out, there’s a strong chance they won’t all come back,” the Colonel said, “and that’s just running for scrap and supplies .” Lara stared at him. “So, instead, you live in hiding. You cower down here, away from the world, because you’re too afraid to face Warlord .” Rodriguez surged to his feet, slamming his fists down on the table. Heavy as it was, the entire thing shook. Ronin stood as well. He’d face deactivation — or worse — to protect Lara . “Every person that’s died under my watch is another I have to carry with me forever! I can ask them to risk their lives for our own, for our survival, to keep their friends and family safe. But you want me to put all of our people at risk to go to war on behalf of some strangers ?” “What will you do when there are too many of you to fit in this place?” Lara asked, surprisingly calm. “When you’re so packed in that you can’t keep up with supplies and food? You’re trapped down here, and he’s as much your master as he is Cheyenne’s if you won’t go out and face this .” Lara rose, hand on the table for support, eyes steady on Rodriguez. “Will you deny couples children and kill their babies? Will you murder anyone who doesn’t follow the rules? Will you refuse someone food, because they aren’t valuable enough to you ?” “Our crops are struggling more and more. It’s a miracle they’ve lasted this long,” said Nancy. “A few more generations, and they won’t be viable .” “We have other plans in place to—” Dave began . “It may be decades before we have the breakthroughs to even begin that kind of genetic modification,” Will said. “Our children and grandchildren may not have that much time .” “There are resources in Cheyenne,” Ronin offered, not looking away from Rodriguez, who stared down at the tabletop. “Warlord has kept a number of fields producing crops, in addition to cattle and goats. Plus, there are production facilities on the south side of town that are still in some working order .” “With those, we can — ” “Enough,” Rodriguez commanded. The room fell silent again as he lowered himself into his seat, nostrils flared . After several moments, Lara sat down. Ronin followed suit. There was a defeated look on her face . Seconds ticked away on Ronin’s internal clock, stretching over a minute . “Is that what he’s doing over there?” Rodriguez finally asked, looking up at Lara . “ What ?” “Killing babies. Murdering anyone that doesn’t obey. Letting people starve. Is that what Warlord is doing in Cheyenne ?” She nodded. “We’re his playthings. He keeps us around until we’re not interesting anymore, or we’re too much trouble. We need his permission to have children, and if a couple has more than one…the baby is killed. He doesn’t allow anyone to grow enough food to survive. The entire settlement has to share a single water pump, and he shuts off the flow at his whim .” Lara found Ronin’s hand again, twining their fingers. “Relationships between humans and bots are forbidden. My sister was taken in by a synth, and when he found out about it, he beat her to death and tore the bot to pieces. He found out about me and Ronin not long after, and hunted us across the Dust like animals.” She took a steadying breath and squeezed Ronin’s hand. “There is no hope in Cheyenne. Only him .” “This is not something we have the resources to fix, Jack,” said Dave . For a long while, Rodriguez scrutinized Lara. “It’s not something we can afford to ignore any more, David,” he finally said . “They’re already using our resources without any sort of payment. Already burdening our people. Now we’re going to risk our lives for them? That’s not the kind of decision that’s going to keep this place running through the years .” “If there’s a viable alternative to this place,” said Captain Cooper, leaning forward beside Nancy, “it’s something we need to consider. The Andersons say we’re on limited time as it is, and all of us know people aren’t meant to live in holes like this .” “We need sunlight,” Nancy added. “Even
